# QA Plan: Sprint 04 — Stealth AI Foundation

**Date**: 2026-05-02
**Generated by**: /qa-plan
**Scope**: 16 stories across 3 systems (Stealth AI, Input, Player Character)
**Engine**: Godot 4.6 (GdUnit4 framework)
**Sprint File**: `production/sprints/sprint-04-stealth-ai-foundation.md`
**Test runner**: `godot -s -d res://addons/gdUnit4/bin/GdUnitCmdTool.gd --ignoreHeadlessMode -a tests/unit -a tests/integration --headless`
**Baseline**: 423/423 PASS, exit 0

---

**Type breakdown**: 10 Logic + 6 Integration. Zero pure UI / Visual-Feel / Config-Data stories this sprint.

**Estimated automated test count**: ~100–130 new test functions (range based on Sprint 02 + 03 average per story).

**Estimated test suite delta**: 423 → ~525–555 PASS post-sprint.

---

## Automated Tests Required

### SAI-001 Guard node scaffold — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/guard_scaffold_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `class_name Guard extends CharacterBody3D` declaration present
- `collision_layer = PhysicsLayers.MASK_AI` post-`_ready()`
- `collision_mask = PhysicsLayers.MASK_WORLD | PhysicsLayers.MASK_PLAYER` post-`_ready()` (no bare int literals)
- Scene structure: `NavigationAgent3D`, `VisionCone: Area3D`, `HearingPoller: Node`, `Perception: Node`, `DialogueAnchor: Node3D`, `OutlineTier: MeshInstance3D` children present
- `VisionCone.shape` is `SphereShape3D` with radius `VISION_MAX_RANGE_M` (default 18.0 m)
- Body-entered handler rejects bodies not in groups `"player"` or `"dead_guard"`
- `OutlineTier.set_tier(mesh, OutlineTier.MEDIUM)` called on guard mesh; uses `material_overlay`
- `actor_id: StringName` exported
- Initial state: `current_alert_state == AlertState.UNAWARE`; both accumulators `0.0`

**Edge cases**: scene re-instantiation idempotency; orphan-free teardown; layer-bit-clearing (no extra bits beyond MASK_AI).

**Estimated test count**: ~9 functions (one per AC).

---

### SAI-002 Enums + signals — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/sai_signals_purity_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- Six SAI-domain signals declared on `Events.gd` per ADR-0002: `alert_state_changed`, `actor_became_alerted`, `actor_lost_target`, `takedown_performed`, `guard_incapacitated`, `guard_woke_up`
- Each signal has the expected payload type signature (typed args, no Variant-leakage except documented exceptions)
- Four enums declared on `StealthAI` class (NEVER `Events.gd`): `AlertState`, `Severity`, `AlertCause`, `TakedownType`
- `Events.gd` structural purity preserved (no enum declarations introduced; no `: Variant` additions beyond `setting_changed`)
- EventLogger handler count incremented to subscribe to all 6 new signals (37 → 43 subscribers, depending on prior count)

**Edge cases**: signal-emission round-trip with payload type-check; enum value stability (don't reorder previously-shipped values).

**Estimated test count**: ~10 functions.

---

### SAI-003 RaycastProvider DI + perception cache — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/perception_cache_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `IRaycastProvider` interface (or duck-typed protocol) accepted via constructor injection
- Cold-start cache returns `null` / empty before first poll
- 10 Hz tick produces deterministic cached samples
- Cache invalidation on actor move / target move
- Test seam allows fault injection of synthetic raycast results

**Edge cases**: zero raycast budget; mid-poll target-frees; concurrent cache reads.

**Estimated test count**: ~8 functions.

---

### SAI-004 F.1 sight fill formula — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/sight_fill_f1_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- F.1 implemented per `design/gdd/stealth-ai.md` §F.1: `sight_fill = base × range_falloff × state_mult × body_factor`
- 25-row parametrized test covering: distance × silhouette × alert state matrix
- Linear range falloff to 18 m (boundary)
- State multipliers (UNAWARE 1.0, SUSPICIOUS x.x, SEARCHING x.x, COMBAT x.x — read from GDD)
- Body factor by silhouette height bucket (standing / crouching / prone)
- Cache write path verified per AC

**Edge cases**: zero distance; >18 m clamp; null target.

**Estimated test count**: ~12–15 functions (parametrized).

---

### SAI-005 F.5 thresholds + state escalation — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/state_escalation_f5_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- T_SUSPICIOUS = 0.3, T_SEARCHING = 0.6, T_COMBAT = 0.95 thresholds applied
- 19-edge state-transition matrix exhaustively (per GDD §State Machine):
  - UNAWARE → SUSPICIOUS (combined ≥ 0.3)
  - SUSPICIOUS → UNAWARE (Pillar 3 reversibility)
  - SUSPICIOUS → SEARCHING (≥ 0.6)
  - SEARCHING → SUSPICIOUS / UNAWARE
  - SEARCHING → COMBAT (≥ 0.95)
  - COMBAT → SEARCHING / SUSPICIOUS / UNAWARE (Pillar 3)
  - UNCONSCIOUS / DEAD terminal entry edges
  - WAKE_UP edge UNCONSCIOUS → SUSPICIOUS (post-VS-deferred — assert *not* invoked in VS scope)
- Combined score formula = max(sight_fill, sound_fill) per GDD
- `alert_state_changed` emission on every transition with correct payload

**Edge cases**: simultaneous threshold cross; zero-score floor; combat-while-already-combat (no re-emit).

**Estimated test count**: ~19 functions (one per edge) + ~5 score-combination functions.

---

### SAI-006 Patrol + investigate behavior — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/feature/stealth_ai/patrol_investigate_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `PatrolController` follows waypoint spline at default walk speed
- On SUSPICIOUS, guard breaks patrol and walks to last-known-target position
- On loss-of-cue, guard returns to nearest patrol waypoint
- `NavigationAgent3D.set_target_position()` called with expected coords
- Integration test loads Plaza section + spawns Guard + asserts position deltas after N physics ticks

**Edge cases**: blocked path (NavigationAgent3D failure mode); target unreachable; mid-patrol death.

**Estimated test count**: ~8 functions.

**Manual evidence required**: Plaza guard demonstrates patrol → suspicion-break → investigate → return loop in editor playthrough. Capture as `production/qa/evidence/sai-plaza-guard-evidence.md`.

---

### SAI-007 F.3 accumulator decay + de-escalation timers — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/decay_f3_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- F.3 per-state decay rate table (UNAWARE / SUSPICIOUS / SEARCHING / COMBAT) applied per tick
- De-escalation timer constant per GDD (e.g., SUSPICIOUS holds for N seconds before decay engages)
- Pillar 3 full-reversibility matrix: starting from any non-terminal state, with no positive input, the accumulator drops to 0.0 and state returns to UNAWARE within bounded time
- Decay does not fire in UNCONSCIOUS / DEAD terminal states

**Edge cases**: decay during state transition; floating-point underflow at near-zero; timer reset on new positive input.

**Estimated test count**: ~10–12 functions.

---

### SAI-008 alert_state_changed audio subscriber — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/feature/stealth_ai/alert_audio_subscriber_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- AudioManager subscribes to `Events.alert_state_changed` at boot
- Severity-gated stinger fires on COMBAT entry only (not on SUSPICIOUS / SEARCHING)
- Music duck applied on SUSPICIOUS / SEARCHING / COMBAT (per GDD audio integration §)
- Music restored on de-escalation back to UNAWARE
- Idempotent re-subscribe guard (double-init does not double-fire)

**Edge cases**: signal fires before AudioManager `_ready()`; subscriber disconnect on `_exit_tree()`; severity 0 (no stinger).

**Estimated test count**: ~7 functions.

---

### SAI-009 Forbidden pattern fences — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/feature/stealth_ai/sai_forbidden_patterns_test.gd`
**What to test** (grep-based static analysis):
- `sai_subscribing_to_player_footstep` — no Stealth AI file connects to `Events.player_footstep` (must use `get_noise_event()` polling per ADR boundary)
- `events_with_state_or_methods` — no SAI enum (`AlertState`, `Severity`, `AlertCause`, `TakedownType`) declared in `events.gd`
- Optional: confirm SAI files don't import `player_character.gd` directly (they consume the GDD-spec'd noise + silhouette interfaces, not the implementation)

**Edge cases**: comment-only mentions of forbidden tokens (must be ignored); file-rename robustness.

**Estimated test count**: ~3–4 grep guards.

---

### SAI-010 Performance budget + integration — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/feature/stealth_ai/sai_perf_budget_test.gd`
**Perf harness**: `prototypes/verification-spike/sai_perf_load.tscn` (or extend `perf_synthetic_load.tscn` per ADR-0008 evidence pattern)
**What to test**:
- 1 Plaza guard at full perception poll (10 Hz vision + hearing) measured against ADR-0008 Stealth-AI sub-budget
- Frame-time spike absent during cache invalidation
- NavigationAgent3D `set_target_position` cost measured per call
- Integration test sets up scene, runs N physics ticks, asserts no warning / error emit, exits cleanly

**Edge cases**: 5-guard sweep (extrapolation curve); guard offscreen (cull-aware perf); save-load round-trip during patrol.

**Manual evidence**: `production/qa/evidence/sai-perf-budget-2026-05-XX.md` documenting measured ms/frame on dev hardware. Per ADR-0008 status (Accepted with deferred numerical verification), pass/fail vs Iris Xe target deferred until target hardware exists.

**Estimated test count**: ~5 integration functions + 1 manual evidence doc.

---

### IN-003 Context routing + dual-focus dismiss — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/input/context_routing_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- GAMEPLAY → MENU push fires `Events.ui_context_changed(MENU, GAMEPLAY)`
- MENU pop returns to GAMEPLAY; signal fires inverse direction
- `ui_cancel` action dismisses topmost context (universal-back per ADR-0004)
- `_unhandled_input` handlers in player code drop events when `InputContext.is_active(GAMEPLAY) == false`
- Stack discipline: LIFO push/pop, never empty (always ends at GAMEPLAY)

**Edge cases**: double-push of same context; pop-when-only-GAMEPLAY (no-op); cross-context event-consume order-of-operations.

**Estimated test count**: ~8 functions.

---

### IN-004 Anti-pattern CI enforcement + debug action gating — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/input/input_anti_pattern_test.gd` + `tools/ci/check_input_anti_patterns.sh`
**What to test**:
- `direct_input_global_query` — no `_unhandled_input` handler queries `Input.is_action_*` without first calling `InputContext.is_active(...)` (grep guard)
- `unregistered_action` — no `Input.is_action_*` calls a string literal not present in `InputActions` constants (grep + cross-reference)
- `cross_context_event_consumption` — no handler consumes an event without context check (grep guard)
- Debug actions (3 constants) provably absent from `InputMap` in non-debug runs (existing IN-001 test already covers this; assert it still holds)

**Edge cases**: comment-only false positives must not flag; multi-line `_unhandled_input` bodies handled.

**Estimated test count**: ~4–5 functions + 1 CI shell script.

---

### IN-005 Edge-case discipline — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/input/edge_case_discipline_test.gd` (single file with 4 sub-cases)
**What to test**:
- **Order of operations**: `_unhandled_input` runs *after* InputContext gate; gate-blocked events do NOT propagate to `_input` peer methods
- **Mouse mode lifecycle**: `Input.MOUSE_MODE_CAPTURED` correct in GAMEPLAY; switches to `MOUSE_MODE_VISIBLE` on MENU push; restores CAPTURED on MENU pop
- **Held-key release**: keys held during context push fire a synthetic release on the popped context (no ghost-WASD walking after pause)
- **Frame-boundary safety**: input event arrives mid-`_physics_process` does not corrupt InputContext stack

**Edge cases**: Esc held through multiple pushes; mouse-capture-failure fallback (Linux Wayland edge case).

**Estimated test count**: ~12 functions across 4 sub-cases.

---

### IN-006 Runtime rebinding API — VS scope — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/input/runtime_rebinding_test.gd` + unit
**What to test**:
- `InputMap.action_erase_events(action) + action_add_event(action, new_event)` round-trip
- New binding persists to `user://settings.cfg [input]` section per ADR-0003
- Restored on game launch via Settings init pass
- Locked actions (`ui_cancel`, `interact`, `pause`) cannot be unbound (validation guard)
- Conflict detection: rebinding to an action's existing key clears the old assignment

**Edge cases**: rebind to invalid event (e.g., unknown joystick); persistence write failure; concurrent rebind during gameplay.

**Estimated test count**: ~10 functions + temp-file teardown helpers.

---

### IN-007 LOADING context gate integration — Integration
**Test file**: `tests/integration/core/input/loading_gate_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- LSS push(LOADING) at transition step 1; pop at transition step 13
- During LOADING context, gameplay events drop (no movement, no interact, no fire)
- `ui_cancel` does NOT pop LOADING (stuck-on-loading prevention)
- LOADING context blocks save/load shortcut keys (F5 / F9)

**Edge cases**: LOADING entered while MENU stacked above; double-push during nested transitions; transition abort path properly pops LOADING.

**Estimated test count**: ~7 functions.

---

### PC-006 Health system — Logic
**Test file**: `tests/unit/core/player_character/player_health_test.gd`
**What to test**:
- `apply_damage(amount: float)` rounds to int internally (or fixed-point per GDD)
- Health clamped to `[0, max_health]`
- `health_changed(new, old)` signal emits on every change
- Death transition: `current_health == 0` → `current_state = DEAD` → `actor_died` (or equivalent) emit
- Signal emission ORDER: health_changed BEFORE state_changed BEFORE death-domain emit (deterministic per GDD)
- `apply_heal(amount)` works when not DEAD; no-op when DEAD
- DEAD state guards: subsequent `apply_damage` no-ops; movement state machine refuses transitions out of DEAD (post-respawn handled by PC-007 which is post-VS)
- Existing PC-005 interact + PC-003 movement do NOT regress on health-related state introduction

**Edge cases**: damage = 0 (no signal storm); damage > max_health (single-shot kill); negative damage (clamp); heal during damage frame.

**Estimated test count**: ~10–12 functions.

## Manual QA Checklist

### SAI-006 Plaza guard playthrough
**Verification method**: Manual playtest in editor + screenshot capture.
**Who must sign off**: User (project lead).
**Evidence to capture**: Screenshots of UNAWARE → SUSPICIOUS → COMBAT → de-escalate cycle. Save as `production/qa/evidence/sai-plaza-guard-evidence.md`.

Checklist:
- [ ] Capsule guard spawns in Plaza scene at known waypoint
- [ ] Guard walks patrol spline (visible motion between waypoints)
- [ ] Approaching guard with Eve in line-of-sight escalates state through SUSPICIOUS → SEARCHING → COMBAT
- [ ] Backing out of line-of-sight de-escalates state back toward UNAWARE within bounded time (Pillar 3)
- [ ] `Events.alert_state_changed` payload prints to console on every transition (debug log)
- [ ] No guard-stuck-on-geometry deadlock during the cycle

---

### SAI-010 Stealth-AI perf budget evidence
**Verification method**: Run perf harness; record measured frame-time.
**Who must sign off**: performance-analyst (self-review acceptable in solo mode).
**Evidence to capture**: `production/qa/evidence/sai-perf-budget-2026-05-XX.md` documenting:
- Hardware spec (CPU, GPU, RAM)
- Measured ms/frame for 1 Plaza guard at full perception poll
- ADR-0008 Stealth-AI sub-budget target (when defined)
- Pass/fail status (DEFERRED if Iris Xe target hardware unavailable)

Checklist:
- [ ] Perf harness scene loads
- [ ] 1 guard runs full perception poll for ≥30s without crash
- [ ] Frame-time recorded
- [ ] Evidence doc created and linked from SAI-010 story Completion Notes

## Smoke Test Scope

Critical paths to verify before sprint QA hand-off (run via `/smoke-check sprint`):

1. Project loads in Godot 4.6 editor without errors
2. F5 (Play) boots Main.tscn — Plaza loads, mouse captures, Eve spawns
3. WASD + mouse movement still works (no regression from PC-003 / PC-005)
4. F5-quicksave + F9-quickload round-trip still works (no regression from SL-002 / SL-003)
5. Section transition (LSS) still completes (no regression from LS-002)
6. **Plaza guard patrols and reacts to player presence** (new this sprint — SAI-006)
7. **Apply-damage command (debug or test seam) reduces Eve's health and fires `health_changed`** (new this sprint — PC-006)
8. **Opening any context (e.g., debug menu) blocks gameplay input until popped** (new this sprint — IN-003 + IN-005)
9. **Quicksave during a transition is gated to LOADING context** (new this sprint — IN-007)
10. Test suite full run: ≥ 423 + Sprint-04 additions, zero failures, zero flaky, exit 0
11. No new GdUnit4 orphan-node warnings introduced
12. Headless boot (`godot --headless --quit-after 5`) completes without parse / autoload errors

---

## Playtest Requirements

| Story | Playtest Goal | Min Sessions | Target Player Type |
|-------|--------------|--------------|-------------------|
| SAI-006 | Does the patrol → suspicion → combat → reset loop *feel* legible to a player who hasn't seen the GDD? | 1 | User (project lead) |
| SAI-008 | Is the audio stinger *theatrical enough* (Pillar 3) without being annoying? | 1 (combine with SAI-006) | User |

Both playtests are advisory. The user can defer them to the post-sprint
"first slice with final look" milestone — placeholder geometry + no final
audio assets means the playtest cannot fully validate feel anyway. Recommend
deferring to Sprint ~12 when final audio + Eve mesh land.

**Sign-off requirement** (when run): Notes captured as
`production/session-logs/playtest-sprint-04-stealth-ai.md`. Reviewed by user.

## Definition of Done — Sprint 04

A story is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified — via automated test result OR documented manual evidence
- [ ] Test file exists at the specified path for all Logic and Integration stories
- [ ] Manual evidence document exists for SAI-006 and SAI-010 (the two stories with manual evidence requirements)
- [ ] Smoke check passes (`/smoke-check sprint` before sprint close)
- [ ] No regressions introduced (suite delta ≥ 0 PASS, zero new failures)
- [ ] Code reviewed (via `/code-review`)
- [ ] Story file updated to `Status: Complete` (via `/story-done`)

A SPRINT is DONE when ALL of the following are true:

- [ ] All 16 Must-Have stories COMPLETE
- [ ] Test suite ≥ 423 + sprint additions, zero regressions
- [ ] Smoke check PASS or PASS WITH WARNINGS (warnings documented)
- [ ] `/scope-check sprint-04` clean (no IDs added beyond the 16)
- [ ] Tech-debt register ≤ 12 items (currently 7; sprint must not push past 12)
- [ ] `production/sprint-status.yaml` shows all 16 stories `status: done`
- [ ] `production/session-state/active.md` close-out section appended

---

## Test Standards Reminders

Per `.claude/docs/coding-standards.md` Testing Standards:

- **Naming**: `[system]_[feature]_test.gd` for files; `test_[scenario]_[expected]` for functions
- **Determinism**: no random seeds, no time-dependent assertions
- **Isolation**: per-test setup/teardown; no execution-order dependence
- **No hardcoded data**: factory functions or constants, not inline magic numbers (exception: boundary value tests)
- **Independence**: no external APIs, databases, file I/O — use DI / test seams
- **Headless-friendly**: tests must pass under `--ignoreHeadlessMode` flag (UI-driven assertions documented as "manual" only)
- **NEVER skip failing tests** to make CI green — fix the underlying issue or roll back the change

## Stop Conditions (test-related, per roadmap)

- **Test failure or regression** — do not patch by skipping. Surface and root-cause.
- **Flaky test introduced** — quarantine and add to `production/qa/flaky-tests.md` if unavoidable; do not let flaky tests count toward sprint sign-off.
- **Tech-debt past 12 items** — pause for triage before continuing.
- **ADR ambiguity surfaces during test design** — escalate to godot-specialist + surface to user.
